_description: מאגר סטודיו צילום באירופה — Yad Vashem photo studio registry. Each entry is identified when a studio stamp / logo / printed name is found on a catalogued photograph (front mount or back). Build out over time; merge from cataloging sessions.

_field_reference.name: official name as printed/stamped
_field_reference.name_alt: list of alternative spellings, transliterations, or short forms encountered
_field_reference.city: city name in English (modern + historical name if relevant)
_field_reference.country: country name in English using 1939 borders for archival consistency
_field_reference.active_from: year studio was established (string for partial dates like '1880s')
_field_reference.active_to: year closed/destroyed; '?' if unknown; 'present' if still operating
_field_reference.addresses: list of known addresses over time
_field_reference.owner_alt: list of alternate owner names, kin, successors
_field_reference.jewish_owned: true / false / unknown — relevant for Yad Vashem context
_field_reference.fate: what happened to studio/owners during Holocaust if known: destroyed_1939_1945, aryanized_year, owners_perished, owners_emigrated_year, survived, unknown
_field_reference.specialties: list — portraits, weddings, events, ID photos, etc.
_field_reference.stamps_and_marks: list of textual marks, logos, or backstamps that identify this studio
_field_reference.sources: list of references / URLs / archival mentions
_field_reference.yv_photos_count: running count of YV photos linked to this studio
_field_reference.added_by: cataloger name or 'system' for seed entries
_field_reference.verified: true if cross-checked with external source; false if from single photo only

studios.name: Foto Forbert
studios.notes: Pioneer Jewish photography studio in Warsaw. The Forbert family was central to Jewish photographic and early cinema work in Poland between the wars. Leon Forbert perished; his son Adolf Forbert survived (later cinematographer in postwar Poland). Adolf's brother Władysław also documented the war.
sources: POLIN Museum of the History of Polish Jews
Adolf Forbert's filmography is well documented in Polish film archives

studios.name: Atelier Strelisker
studios.notes: Established Vienna studio active in the late Habsburg era through the First Republic. Many existing portraits of Vienna Jewish bourgeoisie families bear this studio's mark. Studio was Aryanized after the Anschluss in March 1938.
